name: GPT-3
brainrotTitle: the scaling hypothesis in human form
brainrotOneliner: 175B parameters. same recipe as GPT-2, just turned every dial to maximum. the original scale-pilled moment.
keyInnovations: Validated the scaling hypothesis at unprecedented scale — more compute = more capability
Demonstrated strong few-shot learning without any fine-tuning
In-context learning as an emergent behavior — not explicitly trained for it
Established the 175B parameter regime as the benchmark for capable language models
brainrotSummary: GPT-3 is the scaling hypothesis made flesh. take GPT-2, multiply everything by 100, train on basically all text that existed, and watch few-shot capabilities emerge from nowhere. no architectural innovation needed — just more. this single model convinced the entire industry that scale was the path. era-defining NPC behavior.

comparisons.gpt2: GPT-3 is just GPT-2 after a long bulk season. same architecture, 1000x the parameters. the glow-up.
comparisons.deepseek-v3: GPT-3 proved scale works. DeepSeek V3 proved you can be smarter about it. different eras.
comparisons.llama2: LLaMA 2 is what happens when Meta decides everyone should have access to a GPT-3 class model. democratization arc.
comparisons.gpt4: GPT-4 took GPT-3's soul and put it through an alignment machine. the glow-up continues.
comparisons.claude3: GPT-3 established what big models can do. Claude 3 is what you get when you spend years making them actually safe and useful.
comparisons.llama3-instruct: GPT-3 is the original scale moment. LLaMA 3 Instruct is the open-source community's answer, 4 years later.
comparisons.mixtral-8x7b: GPT-3 is dense all the way through. Mixtral said that's inefficient and routed the experts instead.
comparisons.grok-1: GPT-3 showed what scale unlocks. Grok-1 is a competitor's answer from the team that built the original.
